Installation","color":"fef2c0","default":false,"description":"Problems installing or compiling NumPy"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-09-04T01:15:03Z","updated_at":"2024-09-04T01:15:03Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Steps to reproduce:

I've created a minimum reproducible example called [sam-numpy-example](https://github.com/Jdldeveloper/sam-numpy-example).

The example above is simple a basic [AWS SAM](https://aws.amazon.com/serverless/sam/) setup along with adding numpy inside an AWS lambda layer. Nothing else is done besides scaffold AWS SAM, add numpy and try to run it with `sam local start-api`.

### Error message:

```shell
Invalid lambda response received: Invalid API Gateway Response Keys: {'errorType', 'errorMessage', 'stackTrace', 'requestId'} in {'errorMessage':  
"Unable to import module 'app': Error importing numpy: you should not try to import numpy from\n        its source directory; please exit the numpy
source tree, and relaunch\n        your python interpreter from there.", 'errorType': 'Runtime.ImportModuleError', 'requestId':                    
'387b7f65-d7fe-48b4-8f51-9d074be50daa', 'stackTrace': []}
```


### Additional information:

I thought this was an AWS SAM issue however I opened an issue ([#7429](https://github.com/aws/aws-sam-cli/issues/7429)). The maintainers reviewed it and said from the SAM side of things, everything looks fine. 

They suggested opening an issue here since the error message coming out is directly numpy. Hopefully I can get some insight on what I could do to fix this, ideally I'd like to develop with SAM but this is a major blocker since I also need numpy.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27338/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/27338/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

### Additional information:

I thought this was an AWS SAM issue however I opened an issue ([#7429](https://github.com/aws/aws-sam-cli/issues/7429)) in their repo first. The maintainers reviewed it and said from the SAM side of things, everything looks fine.
